LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT JOHN DALTON'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT MET HIS BURDEN OF "NEGATING THE PROPRIETY OF VENUE AS LAID ON ALL POSSIBLE GROUNDS." (KARSON INDUSTRIES INC. V. SUP. CT. (1969) 273 CAL.APP.2D 7, 8-9.) DEFENDANT'S MOVING PAPERS DO NOT ADDRESS THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE DEFENDANT'S MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, P. 1, LINES 15-16.) ADDITIONALLY, THE COMPLAINT IS FOUNDED ON A CONTRACT, AS ALL OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE OUT OF DEFENDANTS' ALLEGED BREACH OF THE EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENTS AND FEE PROVISIONS. AS SUCH, PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 395(A), VENUE IS PROPER IN THE COUNTY WHERE THE CONTRACTS WERE ENTERED INTO. PLAINTIFF HAS SUBMITTED EVIDENCE THAT THE CONTRACTS WERE ENTERED INTO IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY. (DECLARATION OF ROBIN KAY, 6-9.) DEFENDANT HAS SUBMITTED NO EVIDENCE REFUTING THIS CONTENTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. DEFENDANT JASON OLIVER'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT JASON L. OLIVER ("OLIVER") HAS NOT MET HIS BURDEN OF MAKING A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING THAT THE COMPLAINT ARISES OUT OF AN ACT IN FURTHERANCE OF HIS RIGHT TO PETITION OR FREE SPEECH. (CCP 425.16.) THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT DETERMINES WHETHER THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE APPLIES. (HYLTON V. FRANK E. ROGOZIENSKI, INC. (2009) 177 CAL.APP.4TH 1264, 1272.) THE GRAVAMEN OF THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT AGAINST OLIVER IS THAT HE BREACHED A WRITTEN OR ORAL AGREEMENT TO SPLIT FEES WITH PHILIP E. KAY. ALTHOUGH THE FEES WERE AWARDED IN A JUDICIAL PROCEEDING, THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT DISPUTE THE AMOUNT OF FEES THAT WERE AWARDED. HOW THE FEES SHOULD BE SPLIT BETWEEN THE ATTORNEYS DOES NOT "RELATE TO THE SUBSTANTIVE ISSUES IN THE LITIGATION." (NEVILLE V. CHUDACOFF (2008) 160 CAL.APP.4TH 1255, 1266.) THE COMPLAINT ARISING FROM HOW THE FEES SHOULD BE SPLIT DOES NOT AFFECT OLIVER'S CLIENTS' ABILITY TO SEEK REDRESS IN COURT. THE COURT ALREADY AWARDED THE SUBSTANTIVE RELIEF SOUGHT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JASON OLIVER'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE IS DENIED. "IT IS THE MOVING DEFENDANT'S BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE PLAINTIFF'S VENUE SELECTION IS NOT PROPER UNDER ANY OF THE STATUTORY GROUNDS." (FONTAINE V. SUPERIOR COURT (2009) 175 CAL.APP.4TH 830, 836; SEE ALSO WEIL & BROWN, CAL. PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROC. BEFORE TRIAL (THE RUTTER GROUP), PAR. 3:574-75.) DEFENDANT SETS FORTH NO EVIDENCE IN HIS MOVING PAPERS THAT THE ALLEGED FEE AGREEMENT OR FEE-SPLITTING AGREEMENT GIVING RISE TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION WAS NOT ENTERED INTO IN SAN FRANCISCO. FURTHER, ALTHOUGH NO DEFENDANTS RESIDE IN SAN FRANCISCO, WHERE THERE IS A MORE SPECIFIC VENUE STATUTE, THE GENERAL RULE ABOUT VENUE BEING PROPER IN THE COUNTY OF A DEFENDANT'S RESIDENCE IS SUBORDINATED. (BROWN V. SUP. CT. (1984) 37 CAL.3D 477, 483.) THE SPECIFIC VENUE STATUTE FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT ACTIONS CONTROLS. (CCP SEC. 395(A).) PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Louis H. Castoria, Esq. of Wilson Elser Moskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP, Tel. 415.625.9370, for defendant and moving party;
Thomas J. D'Amato, Esq. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, Tel. 415.788.1900, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. DEFENDANT JASON OLIVER's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied. Defendant Jason L. Oliver ("Oliver") has not met his burden of making a prima facie showing that the complaint arises out of an act in furtherance of his right to petition or free speech. (CCP 425.16.) The gravamen of the complaint determines whether the Anti-SLAPP statute applies. (Hylton v. Frank E. Rogozienski, Inc. (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1264, 1272.) The gravamen of the allegations in the complaint against Oliver is that he breached a written or oral agreement to split fees with Philip E. Kay. Although the fees were awarded in a judicial proceeding, the complaint does not dispute the amount of fees that were awarded. How the fees should be split between the attorneys does not "relate to the substantive issues in the litigation." (Neville v. Chudacoff (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1255, 1266.) The complaint arising from how the fees should be split does not affect Oliver's clients' ability to seek redress in court. The court already awarded the substantive relief sought. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Mtn To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar May-08-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Louis H. Castoria, Esq. of Wilson Elser Moskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP, Tel. 415.625.9370, for defendant and moving party;
Thomas J. D'Amato, Esq. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, Tel. 415.788.1900, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. After hearing, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JASON OLIVER's Motion To Transfer Venue is denied. "It is the moving defendant's burden to demonstrate that the plaintiff's venue selection is not proper under any of the statutory grounds." (Fontaine v. Superior Court (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 830, 836; see also Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civil Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group), par. 3:574-75.) Defendant sets forth no evidence in his moving papers that the alleged fee agreement or fee-splitting agreement giving rise to the breach of contract cause of action was not entered into in San Francisco. Further, although no defendants reside in San Francisco, where there is a more specific venue statute, the general rule about venue being proper in the county of a defendant's residence is subordinated. (Brown v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 37 Cal.3d 477, 483.) The specific venue statute for breach of contract actions controls. (CCP sec. 395(a).) Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Change Venue
Off Calendar Jan-30-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: SIMONE MCCORMICK, ESQ., 415.788.1900, FOR PLANTIFF ROBIN KAY.
JOHN DALTON, 858.720.8422, DEFENDANT IN PROPRIA PERSONA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT JOHN DALTON'S Motion For Change of Venue is denied. Defendant has not met his burden of "negating the propriety of venue as laid on all possible grounds." (Karson Industries Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1969) 273 Cal.App.2d 7, 8-9.) Defendant's moving papers do not address the breach of contract cause of action. (See Defendant's Memorandum of Points and Authorities, p. 1, lines 15-16.) Additionally, the complaint is founded on a contract, as all of the causes of action arise out of Defendants' alleged breach of the employment agreements and fee provisions. As such, pursuant to CCP sec. 395(a), venue is proper in the county where the contracts were entered into. Plaintiff has submitted evidence that the contracts were entered into in San Francisco County. (Declaration of Robin Kay, 6-9.) Defendant has submitted no evidence refuting this contention. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
